abort.mqtt_not_configured: MQTT integration is not configured in Home Assistant.
abort.unknown: Config entry missing — try again from the integrations list.

user.title: TFT Dashboard — main settings
user.description:
This integration **does not replace** Home Assistant’s MQTT setup — it only publishes selected entity states to your broker so the ESP32 can read them.

**Quick start:**
1. In HA: **Settings → Devices & services → MQTT** — the broker must be configured (often the **Mosquitto** add-on). ESP32 and HA must use the **same broker IP** and the same credentials if your broker requires a login.
2. Add this integration (**Add integration** → TFT Dashboard). Keep the prefix `tft/` unless you changed it in the **ESP web config page** — then **both sides must match exactly**.
3. On the integration card, choose **Add entity** and pick e.g. a sensor from the list — no MQTT syntax or YAML required.

If values never update on the display, first check **Developer tools → States** in HA to confirm the entity shows a sensible state.

data_description.mqtt_prefix: Start of **every** topic where the entity state is published, e.g. `tft/` → full path `tft/my_sensor`. Must end with one `/` (added automatically if missing). Use letters, digits, `_` and `-` between slashes; no `//` or spaces.
data_description.retain: The broker stores the last message — after a reboot the ESP immediately sees the last value even if nothing changed.
data_description.publish_on_start: When Home Assistant starts or the integration reloads, values are pushed **right away**, so the TFT does not wait for the first temperature change (etc.).

reconfigure.title: TFT Dashboard — integration options
reconfigure.description:
MQTT prefix in use: `{prefix}`

**Where in HA?** Usually: **TFT Dashboard** card → **⋮** → **Options** (same dialog). If you do not see **Options**, use **Configure** on the integration card.

**Recorder** is HA’s built-in history database — TFT charts can read from it. A shorter refresh interval means fresher charts but more database work.

**Entity limits (20 / 64) and history slots (5 / 12)** — see **Relaxed limits** below and the full explanation under integration **Options** (⋮ → Options).

Changing the prefix later is uncommon: it must still **match** whatever you type into topics on the ESP web UI.

data.relaxed_limits: Higher limits for entities and Recorder history

data_description.relaxed_limits:
**Why limits exist:** each added entity is its own MQTT stream — the ESP has finite RAM and CPU; too many topics at once can destabilise the display. A **history slot** is used by an entity with **history from Recorder** enabled (charts); every refresh queries HA’s database — the cap protects Recorder and CPU.

**Default (this toggle off):** max. **20** entities and **5** history slots at once.

**When enabled:** max. **64** entities and **12** slots — more MQTT traffic and HA load.

**How to “turn off” higher limits:** turn this off and save. **First** remove excess entities from the integration card (below 20) and disable Recorder history on some charts (below 5 slots); otherwise you still exceed the safe default.
data_description.history_poll_interval: How often to **pull full history from HA** for entities where you enabled “history from Recorder”. Plain sensors without charts are barely affected.

error.prefix_invalid_chars: Prefix must end with / and use non-empty segments (letters, digits, _ -), no '//' or empty segments.
error.prefix_too_long: Prefix cannot be longer than 64 characters.

init.title: TFT Dashboard — advanced options
init.description:
**Limits — why they exist:** not a global Home Assistant rule — this integration sets them to **protect the ESP32** (number of MQTT subscriptions and RAM) and **Home Assistant** (Recorder reads for charts). More entities and history slots mean more broker traffic and more CPU/database work on each refresh.

**Default (safer):** up to **20** entities and **5** concurrent **history slots** (one slot = one entity with **history from Recorder** on a line/bar chart or energy price).

**Relaxed mode:** enable **Relaxed limits** below — up to **64** entities and **12** slots. Only enable if you need it; you can **disable it anytime** (Options → off → Save). Before disabling, **reduce** entities on the integration card below 20 and history slots below 5 so you fit the default caps.

**Interval:** how often to refresh full history for chart entities (Recorder-enabled).

**Beginner note:** a history slot is one chart with “from Recorder”. Without charts these options rarely matter.

data.relaxed_limits: Higher limits for entities and Recorder history

data_description.relaxed_limits: **On** = higher caps (64 / 12). **Off** = default caps (20 / 5) — lower load. Turning off only makes sense once you actually fit the lower numbers (remove entities from the integration card or disable “history from Recorder” on some charts).
data_description.history_poll_interval: Shorter = fresher ESP charts, more Recorder database queries.

abort.entity_limit_reached: Entity limit reached: **20** in default mode or **64** with **Relaxed limits** enabled (TFT Dashboard card → ⋮ → **Options**). **What to do:** remove one of the entities already added to this integration **or** turn on *Relaxed limits* in Options and save — the cap becomes 64.

user.title: Step 1 — Select entity
user.description:
Pick **any entity** you already have in Home Assistant (e.g. a Wi‑Fi sensor temperature). Open the dropdown — if something is missing, add that device’s integration first under **Settings → Devices & services**.

**Entity cap:** {entity_count} of {entity_max} used. **{entity_max}** depends on integration **Options** (⋮ → Options): *Relaxed limits* **off** = max. 20 entities, **on** = max. 64. **Lower cap** = less MQTT and ESP load; higher only when you truly need more tiles on the TFT.

role.title: Step 2 — What is this entity for?
role.description:
Entity: **{entity_id}**

This **does not change** the entity in HA — it only tells this integration **how** to publish it over MQTT (e.g. simple number vs chart).

Choose a type from the list (**Sensor** for a basic tile, **Line chart** for history). You can remove the sub-entry later and add the entity again with a different role.

suffix.title: Step 3 — Confirm settings

data_description.suffix: Last part of the MQTT path (after your `tft/` prefix). Letters, digits, `_`, `-`, `/` only — max 64 characters. You can usually keep the suggested value.
data_description.history_enabled: Turn on for **line/bar charts** or **energy price**. Off = the TFT only shows the current value whenever the HA state changes.
data_description.history_from_recorder: **Yes** = periodically pull past values from HA (fuller chart). **No** = chart points only when the entity state changes. **Yes** consumes one **slot** from the limit (see text above the form).
data_description.history_hours: How many hours of HA history to show (1–24). More hours = more points and heavier queries.
data_description.history_hourly: **Yes** = one sample per hour (easy to read). **No** = even downsampling across the window.

options.weather: Weather and forecast (weather.* — often OpenWeatherMap in HA)

error.suffix_duplicate: This suffix is already used by another entity.
error.suffix_invalid_chars: Suffix may only contain letters, digits and _ - / characters.
error.suffix_too_long: Suffix cannot be longer than 64 characters.
error.entity_not_found: Selected entity does not exist in Home Assistant.
error.history_limit_reached: **History slot** limit reached: **5** (default) or **12** (with **Relaxed limits** in Options). One slot = one entity with **history from Recorder** on a chart or energy price. **What to do:** edit the entity and turn off Recorder history **or** remove another entity that uses a slot **or** enable *Relaxed limits* (⋮ → Options) and save.
error.role_weather_mismatch: Weather role applies only to weather.* entities
error.role_binary_mismatch: Binary role applies only to binary_sensor, input_boolean, or switch
error.role_numeric_mismatch: This role needs a numeric entity (sensor, number, input_number)
error.role_requires_numeric_state: Entity state must be numeric (or unknown/unavailable on first setup)
error.history_requires_numeric: Recorder history needs an entity with a numeric state
error.history_hours_invalid: Enter a whole number of hours from 1 to 24
